title: rubus stramineus
intro: Rubus stramineus, also known as the straw thimbleberry, is a species of thimbleberry in the rose family native to North America. It is a deciduous shrub with edible fruits that have been used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rubus stramineus include its potential to reduce oxidative stress, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of Rubus stramineus can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a treatment for various health conditions.
